name: PReM: Learning What to Preserve and When to Refresh for Context Compression

description: arXiv:2607.14327v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Efficient long-context inference is not only about reducing memory cost, but also about keeping useful contextual evidence accessible as generation proceeds. However, existing compression-oriented approaches, such as key-value (KV) cache compression and context compression, often either make an early decision about which contextual information t...
author: Bohan Yu, Lei Shen, Chenxi Zhou, Chen Han, Junlin Liu, Wenbo Su, Yu Cheng, Bo Zheng
